title: Restaurant Server

rationales.taskAutomation: Order-taking and payment are increasingly handled by QR-code menus and tablets, but table-side hospitality, upselling, and adapting to guest needs stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: QR ordering, tableside tablets, and AI-driven POS upsell prompts are mature and already standard in casual dining chains.
rationales.adoption: Fast-casual and mid-tier chains have widely adopted self-order tech; full-service and fine dining still route ordering through servers.
rationales.agenticExposure: The order-payment-fulfillment loop is a clean agent-ownable pipeline once a guest is seated; the hospitality moment itself is not.

tasksAtRisk: Menu question answering via QR-code interfaces
tasksGrowing: Reading the table and personalizing service
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-3031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Working alongside AI ordering and payment kiosks

insight: QR ordering and tableside tablets have already automated the mechanical half of the job: taking orders and running payment. What's left is exactly what pays the tips: reading a table, recovering from a bad moment, and making a stranger feel looked after.
